label: Great Compline (Mega Apodeipnon)
description: Great Compline (Mega Apodeipnon) is the solemn Byzantine night office appointed on weekday evenings during Great Lent and on the eves of Nativity and Theophany. It is substantially longer than Small Compline, with two psalm cycles, the Prayer of Manasseh, a variable canon, and an extended closing prayer sequence. Source witness: orthodoxprayer.org Great Compline. Baseline skeleton only — conditional and variable material deferred.

items.text: The Priest blesses: Through the prayers of our holy Fathers, O Lord Jesus Christ our God, have mercy on us. Amen.

items.label: The Usual Beginning
items.note: Trisagion prayers sequence. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

sections.label: First Psalm Cycle
sections.notes: Psalms 4, 6, and 12 (LXX). In the first week of Great Lent, Psalm 69 precedes. Inter-psalm doxology follows.

items.label: Psalm 69 (first week of Great Lent only)
items.note: Conditional: first week of Great Lent only. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json; conditioned by _resolveGreatComplineSlots().

items.label: Doxology between first and second psalm cycles
items.note: Fixed. Glory… Alleluia × 3… Lord have mercy × 3… Glory…. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

items.label: Doxology between second and third psalm cycles
items.note: Fixed. Glory… Alleluia × 3… Lord have mercy × 3… Glory…. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

sections.label: God is with Us / Day Being Past / Creed
sections.notes: The Isaiah 8 versicle antiphon, the 'Day being past' troparia, the Angelic Hymn (Tone 6), the Creed, and the litanic intercessions. All fixed. Friday: read, not chanted.

items.label: Troparia: The Day Being Past
items.note: Fixed. Three stanzas. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

items.label: Angelic Hymn (Tone 6)
items.note: Fixed. 'The bodiless nature of the Cherubim…'. Friday: read not chanted. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

items.label: Litanic Intercessions (O most holy Lady Theotokos…)

sections.label: Trisagion / Weekday Troparia / Prayer of Basil
sections.notes: Trisagion and Our Father, weekday troparia (variable by day), Lord have mercy × 40, and Prayer of Basil the Great.

items.label: Weekday Troparia (day-dependent)
items.note: Variable: Mon/Wed = Tone 2; Tue/Thu = Tone 8; Feasts = Troparion of the Feast. Resolved by _resolveGreatComplineSlots().

items.label: Lord have mercy × 40 and Prayer of Basil the Great

sections.label: Psalms 50, 101 / Prayer of Manasseh / Sixth-Tone Troparia
sections.notes: Psalms 50 and 101 (LXX), Prayer of Manasseh, Trisagion, second 'O come, let us worship', sixth-tone troparia, Lord have mercy × 40, Prayer of Maradius, and third 'O come, let us worship'.

items.label: O come, let us worship (second occurrence)
items.note: Fixed reopener before Psalms 50 and 101. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

items.label: O come, let us worship (third occurrence — before sixth-tone troparia)
items.note: Fixed reopener before sixth-tone troparia. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

items.label: Troparia in Sixth Tone (Have mercy on us, O Lord…)
Lord have mercy × 40 and Prayer of Saint Maradius
O come, let us worship (third occurrence)
items.note: Fixed reopener before Psalms 69 and 142. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

sections.label: Psalm 142 / Small Doxology / Canon
sections.notes: Psalm 69 (omitted first week), Psalm 142, Small Doxology (read), and the canon. The canon is variable: first week = Great Canon of St. Andrew (Triodion); all other weeks = Menaion or Octoechos canon.

items.label: Psalm 69 (omitted in first week of Great Lent)
items.note: Conditional: omitted in first week of Great Lent. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json; conditioned by _resolveGreatComplineSlots().

items.label: Canon
items.note: Variable. First week of Great Lent: Great Canon of St. Andrew. All other weeks: Menaion or Octoechos canon. Rubric-safe placeholder at baseline. Resolved by _resolveGreatComplineSlots().

sections.label: Closing Prayers and Dismissal
sections.notes: Lord of Hosts kontakion (omitted Fridays), Theotokos prayers, Lord have mercy × 40, Prayer of Ephraim (omitted Fridays), closing prayers, day-dependent Theotokion, and dismissal. All deferred or conditionally resolved at baseline.

items.label: O Lord of Hosts (kontakion; omitted on Fridays)
items.note: Conditional: omitted on Friday evenings. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json; conditioned by _resolveGreatComplineSlots().

items.label: Closing Prayer Block
items.note: Fixed sequence: Theotokos prayers, Lord have mercy × 40, You Who at all times, Lord have mercy × 3, More honorable, Through the prayers. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json.

items.label: Prayer of St. Ephraim (omitted on Fridays)
items.note: Conditional: omitted on Friday evenings. Resolved from great-compline-fixed.json; conditioned by _resolveGreatComplineSlots().

items.label: Trisagion, Our Father, and Lord have mercy × 12 (after prostrations)
Save, help, and protect us, O Virgin Theotokos
Supplicatory Prayer to the Most Holy Theotokos
Prayer to our Lord Jesus Christ (Antiochus the Monk)
Closing Theotokion (day-dependent)
items.note: Variable: Mon/Wed/Fri = Joy of all who sorrow (Tone 2); Tue/Thu = Cross Theotokion (Tone 1). Resolved by _resolveGreatComplineSlots().

items.text: Through the prayers of our holy Fathers, O Lord Jesus Christ our God, have mercy on us. Amen.
